annotation.personal_flag: Shoulder-season fare collapse right as Lanzarote's summer crowds thin out and the big August events wrap up — La Graciosa at its most tranquil for a fraction of the August price.
annotation.raw_reasoning: Late September is past peak Canary Islands season: 'the big summer events are done, the crowds thin out' while sea and air temperatures (~25C) remain swim-friendly. Both legs direct. Cheapest fare found across either provider for this route in either window.

notable_events: Tail end of humpback whale season off Príncipe; gravana dry season transitioning toward October rains
annotation.personal_flag: Same humpback whale season for less than half the August fare, right as São Tomé's dry season winds down before October's rains arrive.
annotation.raw_reasoning: Late September is still within the Jul-Oct humpback whale window and the tail of the dry gravana, though skies stay cloudy and early-October rain is arriving by the return date. Meaningfully cheaper than the August window on the same route via a different one-stop/two-stop combination through Lisbon. Genuinely low season pricing, not an anomaly - thin-baseline route (first scan).

notable_events: San Ginés festival (Arrecife, ~Aug 25) falls just after this return date
annotation.personal_flag: Cheapest August option for the same warm, dry, stable Lanzarote weather, narrowly missing the Aug 25 island-wide festival crowds on the return leg.
annotation.raw_reasoning: Direct outbound, one-stop return via Bilbao. August remains high season for the Canaries generally (structural, not event-driven distortion) even though La Graciosa itself has no bars, sunbeds, or built-up tourist infrastructure by nature of its tiny year-round population.

notable_events: San Ginés festival in Arrecife builds toward Aug 25, drawing island-wide crowds
annotation.personal_flag: Peak-season Lanzarote pricing and crowds on the mainland, but La Graciosa itself (no bars, ~700 residents) stays a quiet, car-free escape a short ferry away.
annotation.raw_reasoning: August is structurally the busiest, priciest month in the Canaries with hot stable weather (25-28C, negligible rain) and the San Ginés festival building through the month. La Graciosa's own infrastructure stays minimal regardless, but accommodation books out 2-3 months ahead. Lastminute fare for this exact 14-night window is direct both ways but pricier than Kiwi's overlapping dates, so not selected as the cheapest option.

notable_events: Peak humpback whale migration season off Príncipe (Jul-Oct)
annotation.personal_flag: Peak humpback whale season off Príncipe on a route that's genuinely off the tourism radar — the fare reflects long-haul, low-frequency African connections rather than any crowd premium.
annotation.raw_reasoning: August sits in São Tomé's long dry season (gravana): warm, mostly rain-free, but skies often grey/overcast rather than clear blue. Humpback whales are present in peak numbers. São Tomé & Príncipe remain essentially untouristed year-round, so isolation is high and crowd level low despite the price. Price is structural (2-stop routing via Addis Ababa/Libreville, no direct competition) not event-driven, so not flagged as a distortion; this is a thin-baseline route (first scan).
